190→\t\t\t\tconst marker = MarkerService._toMarker(owner, resource, data);\n   191→\t\t\t\tif (marker) {\n   192→\t\t\t\t\tmarkers.push(marker);\n   193→\t\t\t\t}\n   194→\t\t\t}\n URI, data: IMarkerData): IMarker | undefined {\n   201→\t\tlet {\n   202→\t\t\tcode, severity,\n   203→\t\t\tmessage, source,\n   204→\t\t\tstartLineNumber, startColumn, endLineNumber, endColumn,\n IExtHostWorkspace, extension: IExtensionDescription, dispatcher: Event<FileSystemEvents>, globPattern: string | IRelativePatternDto, options: FileSystemWatcherCreateOptions) {\n    58→\t\tthis._config = 0;\n    59→\t\tif (options.ignoreCreateEvents) {\n    60→\t\t\tthis._config += 0b001;\n    61→\t\t}\n    62→\t\tif (options.ignoreChangeEvents) {\n    63→\t\t\tthis._config += 0b010;\n    64→\t\t}\n    65→\t\tif (options.ignoreDeleteEvents) {\n    66→\t\t\tthis._config += 0b100;\n    67→\t\t}\n    68→\n    69→\t\tconst parsedPattern = parse(globPattern);\n    70→\n    71→\t\t// 1.64.x behaviour change: given the new support to watch any folder\n    72→\t\t// we start to ignore events outside the workspace when only a string\n    73→\t\t// pattern is provided to avoid sending events to extensions that are\n    74→\t\t// unexpected.\n    75→\t\t// https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/3025\n    76→\t\tconst excludeOutOfWorkspaceEvents = typeof globPattern === 'string';\n    77→\n    78→\t\t// 1.84.x introduces new proposed API for a watcher to set exclude\n    79→\t\t// rules. In these cases, we turn the file watcher into correlation\n    80→\t\t// mode and ignore any event that does not match the correlation ID.\n    81→\t\tconst excludeUncorrelatedEvents = options.correlate;\n    82→\n    83→\t\tconst subscription = dispatcher(events => {\n    84→\t\t\tif (typeof events.session === 'number' && events.session !== this.session) {\n    85→\t\t\t\treturn; // ignore events from other file watchers that are in correlation mode\n    86→\t\t\t}\n    87→\n    88→\t\t\tif (excludeUncorrelatedEvents && typeof events.session === 'undefined') {\n    89→\t\t\t\treturn; // ignore events from other non-correlating file watcher when we are in correlation mode\n    90→\t\t\t}\n    91→\n    92→\t\t\tif (!options.ignoreCreateEvents) {\n    93→\t\t\t\tfor (const created of events.created) {\n    94→\t\t\t\t\tconst uri = URI.revive(created);\n    95→\t\t\t\t\tif (parsedPattern(uri.fsPath) && (!excludeOutOfWorkspaceEvents || workspace.getWorkspaceFolder(uri))) {\n    96→\t\t\t\t\t\tthis._onDidCreate.fire(uri);\n    97→\t\t\t\t\t}\n    98→\t\t\t\t}\n    99→\t\t\t}\n   100→\t\t\tif (!options.ignoreChangeEvents) {\n   101→\t\t\t\tfor (const changed of events.changed) {\n   102→\t\t\t\t\tconst uri = URI.revive(changed);\n   103→\t\t\t\t\tif (parsedPattern(uri.fsPath) && (!excludeOutOfWorkspaceEvents || workspace.getWorkspaceFolder(uri))) {\n   104→\t\t\t\t\t\tthis._onDidChange.fire(uri);\n   105→\t\t\t\t\t}\n   106→\t\t\t\t}\n   107→\t\t\t}\n   108→\t\t\tif (!options.ignoreDeleteEvents) {\n   109→\t\t\t\tfor (const deleted of events.deleted) {\n   110→\t\t\t\t\tconst uri = URI.revive(deleted);\n   111→\t\t\t\t\tif (parsedPattern(uri.fsPath) && (!excludeOutOfWorkspaceEvents || workspace.getWorkspaceFolder(uri))) {\n   112→\t\t\t\t\t\tthis._onDidDelete.fire(uri);\n   113→\t\t\t\t\t}\n   114→\t\t\t\t}\n   115→\t\t\t}\n   116→\t\t});\n   117→\n   118→\t\tthis._disposable = Disposable.from(this.ensureWatching(mainContext, workspace, configuration, extension, globPattern, options, options.correlate), this._onDidCreate, this._onDidChange, this._onDidDelete, subscription);\n   119→\t}\n   120→\n   121→\tprivate ensureWatching(mainContext: IMainContext, workspace: IExtHostWorkspace, configuration: ExtHostConfigProvider, extension: IExtensionDescription, globPattern: string | IRelativePatternDto, options: FileSystemWatcherCreateOptions, correlate: boolean | undefined): Disposable {\n   122→\t\tconst disposable = Disposable.from();\n   123→\n   124→\t\tif (typeof globPattern === 'string') {\n   125→\t\t\treturn disposable; // workspace is already watched by default, no need to watch again!\n   126→\t\t}\n   127→\n   128→\t\tif (options.ignoreChangeEvents && options.ignoreCreateEvents && options.ignoreDeleteEvents) {\n   129→\t\t\treturn disposable; // no need to watch if we ignore all events\n   130→\t\t}\n   131→\n   132→\t\tconst proxy = mainContext.getProxy(MainContext.MainThreadFileSystemEventService);\n   133→\n   134→\t\tlet recursive = false;\n   135→\t\tif (globPattern.pattern.includes(GLOBSTAR) || globPattern.pattern.includes(GLOB_SPLIT)) {\n   136→\t\t\trecursive = true; // only watch recursively if pattern indicates the need for it\n   137→\t\t}\n   138→\n   139→\t\tconst excludes = options.excludes ?? [];\n   140→\t\tlet includes: Array<string | IRelativePattern> | undefined = undefined;\n   141→\t\tlet filter: FileChangeFilter | undefined;\n   142→\n   143→\t\t// Correlated: adjust filter based on arguments\n   144→\t\tif (correlate) {\n   145→\t\t\tif (options.ignoreChangeEvents || options.ignoreCreateEvents || options.ignoreDeleteEvents) {\n   146→\t\t\t\tfilter = FileChangeFilter.UPDATED | FileChangeFilter.ADDED | FileChangeFilter.DELETED;\n   147→\n   148→\t\t\t\tif (options.ignoreChangeEvents) {\n   149→\t\t\t\t\tfilter &= ~FileChangeFilter.UPDATED;\n   150→\t\t\t\t}\n   151→\n   152→\t\t\t\tif (options.ignoreCreateEvents) {\n   153→\t\t\t\t\tfilter &= ~FileChangeFilter.ADDED;\n   154→\t\t\t\t}\n   155→\n   156→\t\t\t\tif (options.ignoreDeleteEvents) {\n   157→\t\t\t\t\tfilter &= ~FileChangeFilter.DELETED;\n   158→\t\t\t\t}\n   159→\t\t\t}\n   160→\t\t}\n   161→\n   162→\t\t// Uncorrelated: adjust includes and excludes based on settings\n   163→\t\telse {\n   164→\n   165→\t\t\t// Automatically add `files.watcherExclude` patterns when watching\n   166→\t\t\t// recursively to give users a chance to configure exclude rules\n   167→\t\t\t// for reducing the overhead of watching recursively\n   168→\t\t\tif (recursive && excludes.length === 0) {\n   169→\t\t\t\tconst workspaceFolder = workspace.getWorkspaceFolder(URI.revive(globPattern.baseUri));\n   170→\t\t\t\tconst watcherExcludes = configuration.getConfiguration('files', workspaceFolder).get<IGlobPatterns>('watcherExclude');\n   171→\t\t\t\tif (watcherExcludes) {\n   172→\t\t\t\t\tfor (const key in watcherExcludes) {\n   173→\t\t\t\t\t\tif (key && watcherExcludes[key] === true) {\n   174→\t\t\t\t\t\t\texcludes.push(key);\n   175→\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n   176→\t\t\t\t\t}\n   177→\t\t\t\t}\n   178→\t\t\t}\n   179→\n   180→\t\t\t// Non-recursive watching inside the workspace will overlap with\n   181→\t\t\t// our standard workspace watchers. To prevent duplicate events,\n   182→\t\t\t// we only want to include events for files that are otherwise\n   183→\t\t\t// excluded via `files.watcherExclude`. As such, we configure\n   184→\t\t\t// to include each configured exclude pattern so that only those\n   185→\t\t\t// events are reported that are otherwise excluded.\n   186→\t\t\t// However, we cannot just use the pattern as is, because a pattern\n   187→\t\t\t// such as `bar` for a exclude, will work to exclude any of\n   188→\t\t\t// `<workspace path>/bar` but will not work as include for files within\n   189→\t\t\t// `bar` unless a suffix of `/**` if added.\n   190→\t\t\t// (https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/148245)\n   191→\t\t\telse if (!recursive) {\n   192→\t\t\t\tconst workspaceFolder = workspace.getWorkspaceFolder(URI.revive(globPattern.baseUri));\n   193→\t\t\t\tif (workspaceFolder) {\n   194→\t\t\t\t\tconst watcherExcludes = configuration.getConfiguration('files', workspaceFolder).get<IGlobPatterns>('watcherExclude');\n   195→\t\t\t\t\tif (watcherExcludes) {\n   196→\t\t\t\t\t\tfor (const key in watcherExcludes) {\n   197→\t\t\t\t\t\t\tif (key && watcherExcludes[key] === true) {\n   198→\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tconst includePattern = `${rtrim(key, '/')}/${GLOBSTAR}`;\n   199→\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tif (!includes) {\n   200→\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tincludes = [];\n   201→\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n   202→\n   203→\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tincludes.push(normalizeWatcherPattern(workspaceFolder.uri.fsPath, includePattern));\n   204→\t\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n   205→\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n   206→\t\t\t\t\t}\n   207→\n   208→\t\t\t\t\t// Still ignore watch request if there are actually no configured\n   209→\t\t\t\t\t// exclude rules, because in that case our default recursive watcher\n   210→\t\t\t\t\t// should be able to take care of all events.\n   211→\t\t\t\t\tif (!includes || includes.length === 0) {\n   212→\t\t\t\t\t\treturn disposable;\n   213→\t\t\t\t\t}\n   214→\t\t\t\t}\n   215→\t\t\t}\n   216→\t\t}\n   217→\n   218→\t\tproxy.$watch(extension.identifier.value, this.session, globPattern.baseUri, { recursive, excludes, includes, filter }, Boolean(correlate));\n   219→\n   220→\t\treturn Disposable.from({ dispose: () => proxy.$unwatch(this.session) });\n   221→\t}\n   222→\n   223→\tdispose() {\n   224→\t\tthis._disposable.dispose();\n   225→\t}\n   226→\n   227→\tget onDidCreate(): Event<vscode.Uri> {\n   228→\t\treturn this._onDidCreate.event;\n   229→\t}\n   230→\n   231→\tget onDidChange(): 238→}\n   239→\n   240→interface IExtensionListener<E> {\n   241→\textension: IExtensionDescription;\n   242→\t(e: E): any;\n   243→}\n   244→\n   245→class LazyRevivedFileSystemEvents implements 280→\t) {\n   281→\t\t//\n   282→\t}\n   283→\n   284→\t//--- file events\n   285→\n   286→\tcreateFileSystemWatcher(workspace: IExtHostWorkspace, configProvider: ExtHostConfigProvider, extension: This is the place where file changes from Git operations would be detected.\n\nNow I understand the full flow:1. When a file changes on disk (e.g., from `git checkout`), `IFileService.onDidFilesChange` fires\n2. `MainThreadFileSystemEventService` catches this and sends it to extensions via `$onFileEvent`\n3. Extensions receive the file event and can listen via `workspace.onDidChangeTextDocument` or `workspace.createFileSystemWatcher`\n\nThe problem is that the TypeScript language server extension doesn't automatically re-run diagnostics when files change on disk. It only re-runs diagnostics when:1. A file is edited in the editor\n2. An explicit \"run diagnostics\" command is issued\n\nTo fix this, I need to add a mechanism that triggers diagnostics refresh when files change on disk. The best place to do this would be in the main thread diagnostics system - when files change on disk, we should clear stale diagnostics for those files and trigger re-diagnostics.\n\nHowever, this is tricky because:1. Different extensions provide diagnostics\n2. We can't force extensions to re-run diagnostics just because a file changed\n3. Clear any diagnostics for those files from all owners\n3. Let the extensions re-provide diagnostics if they want to\n\nBut that would mean losing diagnostics momentarily. \n\nI could trigger a synthetic file-saved event when files change on disk to prompt extensions to re-analyze them, or directly clear diagnostics for deleted files while re-running analysis for modified ones. But extensions that provide diagnostics (like TypeScript extension) may not be listening to these file change events, OR they listen but don't properly refresh diagnostics for all file types\n\nThe fix should be to ensure that when files change on disk, we trigger a diagnostics refresh. I can see it creates a \"lazyClientHost\" that manages the TypeScript client. Now I need to understand where diagnostics are actually provided. But I realize I might be overthinking this.\n\nLet me re-read the task description more carefully to understand what exactly needs to be implemented:\n\n\"Check the full diagnostics flow from a text change through the extension host to the Problems view, identify where file system changes should trigger diagnostic updates, and propose where we'd need to add listeners to fix this.\"\n\nThe task is asking me to:\n1. Understand the diagnostics flow\n2. Identify where file system changes should trigger diagnostic updates\n3. Propose/implement where listeners need to be added\n\nI think the fix should be:\n1. When files change on disk (via `IFileService.onDidFilesChange`)\n2. Clear diagnostics for deleted files from the marker service\n3. For changed files, we can either:   - Clear diagnostics and let extensions re-run (but this would lose diagnostics temporarily)   - Or inform extensions about the change so they can update diagnostics\n\nActually, I think the simplest approach that makes sense is:\n\nIn the `MainThreadDiagnostics` class:\n1. Listen to `IFileService.onDidFilesChange`\n2. When files are deleted, call `markerService.remove(owner, deletedUris)` for all owners\n3. This will clear stale diagnostics for deleted files\n4. For changed files, we could clear all diagnostics and let extensions re-provide them, OR we could leave it to extensions to handle (which they should via their file watchers)\n\nActually, thinking about this more... the problem is that when Git switches branches:\n1. Some files might have errors in the old branch but not in the new branch\n2. VS Code still shows the old errors\n3. When you edit the file, the errors refresh\n\nSo the fix is:1. When files change on disk, clear ALL diagnostics for those files (assuming extensions will re-provide them if still valid)2. \n\nWhen a file's content changes on disk, stale diagnostics should be cleared so that if the new branch doesn't have errors, those old markers don't persist. {"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"type":"text","text":"# Simplify: Code Review and Cleanup\n\nReview all changed files for reuse, quality, and efficiency. Fix any issues found.\n\n## Phase 1: Identify Changes\n\nRun `git diff` (or `git diff HEAD` if there are staged changes) to see what changed. If there are no git changes, review the most recently modified files that the user mentioned or that you edited earlier in this conversation.\n\n## Phase 2: Launch Three Review Agents in Parallel\n\nUse the Agent tool to launch all three agents concurrently in a single message. Pass each agent the full diff so it has the complete context.\n\n### Agent 1: Code Reuse Review\n\nFor each change:\n\n1. **Search for existing utilities and helpers** that could replace newly written code. Use Grep to find similar patterns elsewhere in the codebase — common locations are utility directories, shared modules, and files adjacent to the changed ones.\n2. **Flag any new function that duplicates existing functionality.** Suggest the existing function to use instead.\n3. **Flag any inline logic that could use an existing utility** — hand-rolled string manipulation, manual path handling, custom environment checks, ad-hoc type guards, and similar patterns are common candidates.\n\n### Agent 2: Code Quality Review\n\nReview the same changes for hacky patterns:\n\n1. **Redundant state**: state that duplicates existing state, cached values that could be derived, observers/effects that could be direct calls\n2. **Parameter sprawl**: adding new parameters to a function instead of generalizing or restructuring existing ones\n3. **Copy-paste with slight variation**: near-duplicate code blocks that should be unified with a shared abstraction\n4. **Leaky abstractions**: exposing internal details that should be encapsulated, or breaking existing abstraction boundaries\n5. **Stringly-typed code**: using raw strings where constants, enums (string unions), or branded types already exist in the codebase\n\n### Agent 3: Efficiency Review\n\nReview the same changes for efficiency:\n\n1. **Unnecessary work**: redundant computations, repeated file reads, duplicate network/API calls, N+1 patterns\n2. **Missed concurrency**: independent operations run sequentially when they could run in parallel\n3. **Hot-path bloat**: new blocking work added to startup or per-request/per-render hot paths\n4. **Unnecessary existence checks**: pre-checking file/resource existence before operating (TOCTOU anti-pattern) — operate directly and handle the error\n5. **Memory**: unbounded data structures, missing cleanup, event listener leaks\n6. **Overly broad operations**: reading entire files when only a portion is needed, loading all items when filtering for one\n\n## Phase 3: Fix Issues\n\nWait for all three agents to complete. Aggregate their findings and fix each issue directly. If a finding is a false positive or not worth addressing, note it and move on — do not argue with the finding, just skip it.\n\nWhen done, briefly summarize what was fixed (or confirm the code was already clean).\n\n\n## Additional Focus\n\nsrc/vs/workbench/api/browser/mainThreadDiagnostics.ts"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"dfbf868f-06e8-4677-b1b2-759b254ab67e","uuid":"b7875e34-e2a1-42e4-9407-9893ac19e531","isSynthetic":true}

65→\t\t\tif (markers.length > 0) {\n    66→\t\t\t\t// Remove all diagnostics for this file from all owners\n    67→\t\t\t\tconst owners = new Set<string>();\n    68→\t\t\t\tfor (const marker of 478→\t\tthis._state++;\n   479→\t}\n   480→\n   481→\tprivate addItemFirst(item: Item<K, V>): void {\n   482→\t\t// First time Insert\n   483→\t\tif (!this._head && !this._tail) {\n   484→\t\t\tthis._tail = item;\n   485→\t\t} else if (!this._head) {\n   486→\t\t\tthrow new Error('Invalid list');\n   487→\t\t} else {\n   488→\t\t\titem.next = this._head;\n 496→\t\t// First time Insert\n   497→\t\tif (!this._head && !this._tail) {\n   498→\t\t\tthis._head = item;\n   499→\t\t} else if (!this._tail) {\n   500→\t\t\tthrow new Error('Invalid list');\n 509→\tprivate removeItem(item: Item<K, V>): void {\n   510→\t\tif (item === this._head && item === this._tail) {\n   511→\t\t\tthis._head = undefined;\n   512→\t\t\tthis._tail = undefined;\n   513→\t\t}\n   514→\t\telse if (item === this._head) {\n   515→\t\t\t// This can only happen if size === 1 which is handled\n   516→\t\t\t// by the case above.\n   517→\t\t\tif (!item.next) {\n   518→\t\t\t\tthrow new Error('Invalid list');\n   519→\t\t\t}\n   520→\t\t\titem.next.previous = undefined;\n   521→\t\t\tthis._head = item.next;\n   522→\t\t}\n   523→\t\telse if (item === this._tail) 528→\t\t\t}\n   529→\t\t\titem.previous.next = undefined;\n   530→\t\t\tthis._tail = item.previous;\n   531→\t\t}\n   532→\t\telse {\n   533→\t\t\tconst next = item.next;\n   534→\t\t\tconst previous = item.previous;\n   535→\t\t\tif (!next || !previous) {\n   536→\t\t\t\tthrow new Error('Invalid list');\n   537→\t\t\t}\n   538→\t\t\tnext.previous = previous;\n   539→\t\t\tprevious.next = next;\n 547→\t\tif (!this._head || !this._tail) {\n   548→\t\t\tthrow new Error('Invalid list');\n   549→\t\t}\n   550→\t\tif ((touch !== Touch.AsOld && touch !== Touch.AsNew)) {\n   551→\t\t\treturn;\n   552→\t\t}\n   553→\n   554→\t\tif (touch === Touch.AsOld) {\n   555→\t\t\tif (item === this._head) {\n   556→\t\t\t\treturn;\n   557→\t\t\t}\n   558→\n   559→\t\t\tconst next = item.next;\n   560→\t\t\tconst previous = item.previous;\n   561→\n   562→\t\t\t// Unlink the item\n   563→\t\t\tif (item === this._tail) {\n   564→\t\t\t\t// previous must be defined since item was not head but is tail\n   565→\t\t\t\t// So there are more than on item in the map\n   566→\t\t\t\tprevious!.next = undefined;\n   567→\t\t\t\tthis._tail = previous;\n   568→\t\t\t}\n   569→\t\t\telse {\n   570→\t\t\t\t// Both next and previous are not undefined since item was neither head nor tail.\n   571→\t\t\t\tnext!.previous = previous;\n   572→\t\t\t\tprevious!.next = next;\n   573→\t\t\t}\n   574→\n   575→\t\t\t// Insert the node at head\n   576→\t\t\titem.previous = undefined;\n   577→\t\t\titem.next = this._head;\n   578→\t\t\tthis._head.previous = item;\n   579→\t\t\tthis._head = = undefined;\n   601→\t\t\titem.previous = this._tail;\n   602→\t\t\tthis._tail.next = item;\n   603→\t\t\tthis._tail = item;\n   604→\t\t\tthis._state++;\n   605→\t\t}\n   606→\t}\n   607→\n 623→\t\t}\n   624→\t}\n   625→}\n   626→\n   627→abstract class Cache<K, V> extends LinkedMap<K, V> {\n   628→\n   629→\tprotected _limit: number;\n   630→\tprotected _ratio: number;\n   631→\n   632→\tconstructor(limit: number, ratio: number = 1) {\n   633→\t\tsuper();\n   634→\t\tthis._limit = limit;\n   635→\t\tthis._ratio = Math.min(Math.max(0, ratio), 1);\n   636→\t}\n   637→\n 653→\t\tthis.checkTrim();\n   654→\t}\n   655→\n   656→\toverride get(key: K, touch: Touch = Touch.AsNew): V | undefined {\n   657→\t\treturn super.get(key, touch);\n   658→\t}\n   659→\n 695→export class MRUCache<K, V> extends Cache<K, V> {\n   696→\n   697→\tconstructor(limit: number, ratio: number = 1) {\n   698→\t\tsuper(limit, ratio);\n   699→\t}\n   700→\n   701→\tprotected 721→\t\treturn this;\n   722→\t}\n   723→\n   724→\tdelete(value: T): boolean {\n   725→\t\tlet counter = this.map.get(value) || 0;\n   726→\n   727→\t\tif (counter === 0) {\n   728→\t\t\treturn false;\n   729→\t\t}\n   730→\n   731→\t\tcounter--;\n   732→\n   733→\t\tif (counter === 0) {\n   734→\t\t\tthis.map.delete(value);\n   735→\t\t} else {\n   736→\t\t\tthis.map.set(value, counter);\n that allows access both by keys and values.\n   749→ * **NOTE**: values need to be unique.\n   750→ */\n   751→export class BidirectionalMap<K, V> {\n   752→\n   753→\tprivate readonly _m1 = new 775→\t\treturn this._m1.get(key);\n   776→\t}\n   777→\n   778→\tgetKey(value: V): K | undefined {\n   779→\t\treturn this._m2.get(value);\n   780→\t}\n   781→\n   782→\tdelete(key: K): boolean {\n 802→\tvalues(): IterableIterator<V> {\n   803→\t\treturn this._m1.values();\n   804→\t}\n   805→}\n   806→\n   807→export class SetMap<K, V> {\n   808→\n   809→\tprivate map = new Map<K, Set<V>>();\n values);\n   817→\t\t}\n   818→\n   819→\t\tvalues.add(value);\n   820→\t}\n   821→\n   822→\tdelete(key: K, value: V): void {\n   823→\t\tconst values = this.map.get(key);\n   824→\n   825→\t\tif (!values) {\n   826→\t\t\treturn;\n   827→\t\t}\n   828→\n   829→\t\tvalues.delete(value);\n   830→\n   831→\t\tif (values.size === 0) {\n   832→\t\t\tthis.map.delete(key);\n   833→\t\t}\n   834→\t}\n   835→\n   836→\tforEach(key: K, fn: (value: V) => void): void {\n   837→\t\tconst values = this.map.get(key);\n   838→\n   839→\t\tif (!values) {\n   840→\t\t\treturn;\n   841→\t\t}\n   842→\n 850→\t\t}\n   851→\t\treturn values;\n   852→\t}\n   853→}\n   854→\n   855→export function mapsStrictEqualIgnoreOrder(a: Map<unknown, unknown>, b: Map<unknown, unknown>): boolean {\n   856→\tif (a === b) {\n   857→\t\treturn true;\n   858→\t}\n   859→\n   860→\tif (a.size !== b.size) {\n   861→\t\treturn false;\n   862→\t}\n   863→\n   864→\tfor (const [key, value] of a) {\n   865→\t\tif 873→\t\t}\n   874→\t}\n   875→\n   876→\treturn true;\n   877→}\n   878→\n   879→/**\n   880→ * A map that is addressable with 2 separate keys. This is useful in high performance scenarios\n   881→ * where creating a composite key whenever the data is accessed is too expensive.\n   882→ */\n   883→export class TwoKeyMap<TFirst extends string | number, TSecond extends string | number, TValue> {\n   884→\tprivate _data: { [key: string | number]: { [key: string | number]: TValue | undefined } | undefined } = {};\n   885→\n   886→\tpublic set(first: TFirst, second: TSecond, value: TValue): void {\n   887→\t\tif (!this._data[first]) {\n   888→\t\t\tthis._data[first] = {};\n   889→\t\t}\n   890→\t\tthis._data[first as string | number]![second] = value;\n   891→\t}\n   892→\n   893→\tpublic get(first: TFirst, second: TSecond): TValue | undefined {\n   894→\t\treturn this._data[first as string | number]?.[second];\n   895→\t}\n   896→\n   897→\tpublic clear(): void {\n   898→\t\tthis._data = {};\n   899→\t}\n   900→\n   901→\tpublic *values(): IterableIterator<TValue> {\n   902→\t\tfor (const first in this._data) {\n   903→\t\t\tfor (const second in this._data[first]) {\n   904→\t\t\t\tconst value = this._data[first]![second];\n   905→\t\t\t\tif (value) {\n   906→\t\t\t\t\tyield value;\n   907→\t\t\t\t}\n   908→\t\t\t}\n   909→\t\t}\n   910→\t}\n   911→}\n   912→\n   913→/**\n   914→ * A map that is addressable with 3 separate keys. This is useful in high performance scenarios\n   915→ * where creating a composite key whenever the data is accessed is too expensive.\n   916→ */\n   917→export class ThreeKeyMap<TFirst extends string | number, TSecond extends string | number, TThird extends string | number, TValue> {\n   918→\tprivate _data: { [key: string | number]: TwoKeyMap<TSecond, TThird, TValue> | undefined } = {};\n   919→\n   920→\tpublic set(first: TFirst, second: TSecond, third: TThird, value: TValue): void {\n   921→\t\tif (!this._data[first]) {\n   922→\t\t\tthis._data[first] = new TwoKeyMap();\n   923→\t\t}\n   924→\t\tthis._data[first as string | number]!.set(second, third, value);\n   925→\t}\n   926→\n   927→\tpublic get(first: TFirst, second: TSecond, third: TThird): TValue | undefined {\n   928→\t\treturn this._data[first as string | number]?.get(second, third);\n   929→\t}\n   930→\n   931→\tpublic clear(): void {\n   932→\t\tthis._data = {};\n   933→\t}\n   934→\n   935→\tpublic *values(): IterableIterator<TValue> {\n   936→\t\tfor (const first in this._data) {\n   937→\t\t\tfor (const value of this._data[first]!.values()) {\n   938→\t\t\t\tif (value) {\n   939→\t\t\t\t\tyield value;\n   940→\t\t\t\t}\n   941→\t\t\t}\n   942→\t\t}\n   943→\t}\n   944→}\n   945→\n   946→/**\n   947→ * A map that is addressable with 4 separate keys. This is useful in high performance scenarios\n   948→ * where creating a composite key whenever the data is accessed is too expensive.\n   949→ */\n   950→export class FourKeyMap<TFirst extends string | number, TSecond extends string | number, TThird extends string | number, TFourth extends string | number, TValue> {\n   951→\tprivate _data: TwoKeyMap<TFirst, TSecond, TwoKeyMap<TThird, TFourth, TValue>> = new TwoKeyMap();\n   952→\n   953→\tpublic set(first: TFirst, second: TSecond, third: TThird, fourth: TFourth, value: TValue): void {\n   954→\t\tif (!this._data.get(first, second)) {\n   955→\t\t\tthis._data.set(first, second, new TwoKeyMap());\n   956→\t\t}\n   957→\t\tthis._data.get(first, second)!.set(third, fourth, value);\n   958→\t}\n   959→\n   960→\tpublic get(first: TFirst, second: TSecond, third: TThird, fourth: TFourth): TValue | undefined {\n   961→\t\treturn this._data.get(first, second)?.get(third, fourth);\n   962→\t}\n   963→\n   964→\tpublic clear(): void {\n   965→\t\tthis._data.clear();\n   966→\t}\n   967→}\n   968→\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. marker.severity;\n   342→\t}\n   343→\n   344→\t// --- event debounce logic\n   345→\n   346→\tprivate static _merge(all: (readonly URI[])[]): URI[] {\n   347→\t\tconst set = new CRITICAL: Duplicate Code Pattern (Copy-Paste Issue)\n\n**Severity: High**\n\nThe `deleted` (lines 62-75) and `updated` (lines 79-92) handling blocks are **nearly identical**:\n\n```typescript\n// Deleted block (lines 62-75)\nfor (const deletedUri of event.rawDeleted) {\n    const canonicalUri = this._uriIdentService.asCanonicalUri(deletedUri);\n    const markers = this._markerService.read({ resource: canonicalUri });\n    if (markers.length > 0) {\n        const owners = new Set<string>();\n        for (const marker of markers) {\n            owners.add(marker.owner);\n        }\n        for (const owner of owners) {\n            this._markerService.changeOne(owner, canonicalUri, []);\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n// Updated block (lines 79-92) - IDENTICAL LOGIC\nfor (const changedUri of event.rawUpdated) {\n    const canonicalUri = this._uriIdentService.asCanonicalUri(changedUri);\n    const markers = this._markerService.read({ resource: canonicalUri });\n    if (markers.length > 0) {\n        const owners = new Set<string>();\n        for (const marker of markers) {\n            owners.add(marker.owner);\n        }\n        for (const owner of owners) {\n            this._markerService.changeOne(owner, canonicalUri, []);\n        }\n    }\n}\n```\n\n**Issue**: Both blocks perform the exact same operation. They should be extracted into a single helper method to avoid maintenance issues and potential divergence.\n\n**Recommendation**: \n```typescript\nprivate _clearDiagnosticsForUris(uris: URI[]): void {\n    for (const uri of uris) {\n        const canonicalUri = this._uriIdentService.asCanonicalUri(uri);\n        const markers = this._markerService.read({ resource: canonicalUri });\n        if (markers.length > 0) {\n            const owners = new Set<string>();\n            for (const marker of markers) {\n                owners.add(marker.owner);\n            }\n            for (const owner of owners) {\n                this._markerService.changeOne(owner, canonicalUri, []);\n            }\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nprivate _onDidFilesChange(event: { rawAdded: URI[]; rawUpdated: URI[]; rawDeleted: URI[] }): void {\n    this._clearDiagnosticsForUris(event.rawDeleted);\n    this._clearDiagnosticsForUris(event.rawUpdated);\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n### 2. GOOD: State Management and Disposal\n\n**Severity: None - Properly Handled**\n\nThe `_fileListener` is correctly managed:\n- Properly declared as `private readonly _fileListener: IDisposable` (line 21)\n- Subscribed in constructor with bound context (line 32)\n- Disposed in `dispose()` method before clearing active owners (line 37)\n\nThis follows VS Code conventions and prevents memory leaks. The disposal order is also correct—listeners are cleaned up before clearing the active owners set.\n\n---\n\n### 3. CONCERN: Leaky Abstraction - API Pattern\n\n**Severity: Medium**\n\nThe code reads markers and then calls `changeOne()` multiple times per URI. This pattern is correct but verbose:\n\n```typescript\nconst markers = this._markerService.read({ resource: canonicalUri });\n// ... extract owners ...\nfor (const owner of owners) {\n    this._markerService.changeOne(owner, canonicalUri, []);\n}\n```\n\n**Analysis**: Looking at the `IMarkerService` API (in `/workspace/src/vs/platform/markers/common/markers.ts`):\n- `changeOne(owner: string, resource: URI, markers: IMarkerData[]): void` - Updates one resource\n- `changeAll(owner: string, data: IResourceMarker[]): void` - Bulk operation\n\nThe current approach using `changeOne()` in a loop is inefficient. However, examining how `changeAll()` is used in the codebase (line 38, 116), it requires the full resource-marker structure, making it less suitable for this clearing operation.\n\n**Assessment**: The pattern is acceptable but could be optimized. There's no bulk \"clear for resource across all owners\" API, so the loop pattern is necessary.\n\n---\n\n### 4. ERROR HANDLING - Missing\n\n**Severity: Medium**\n\nNo error handling is present. If `_fileService.onDidFilesChange` throws or if marker operations fail, exceptions will propagate:\n\n```typescript\n// Constructor line 32 - No try-catch\nthis._fileListener = this._fileService.onDidFilesChange(this._onDidFilesChange, this);\n\n// Method lines 60-93 - No error handling\nprivate _onDidFilesChange(event: { rawAdded: URI[]; rawUpdated: URI[]; rawDeleted: URI[] }): void {\n    // ... could throw if read() or changeOne() fails\n}\n```\n\n**Issues**:\n1. If `_markerService.read()` throws, the entire batch of file changes is abandoned\n2. If `_markerService.changeOne()` throws mid-loop, some diagnostics are cleared but not others\n3. No logging of errors for debugging\n\n**Recommendation**:\n```typescript\nprivate _onDidFilesChange(event: { rawAdded: URI[]; rawUpdated: URI[]; rawDeleted: URI[] }): void {\n    try {\n        this._clearDiagnosticsForUris(event.rawDeleted);\n        this._clearDiagnosticsForUris(event.rawUpdated);\n    } catch (error) {\n        // Log error but don't crash - file changes should not break diagnostics system\n        console.error('Error clearing diagnostics for file changes:', error);\n    }\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n### 5. POTENTIAL INFINITE LOOP RISK\n\n**Severity: Low**\n\n**Analysis**: The code clears diagnostics when files change, but this does NOT trigger new file change events because:\n- Clearing diagnostics modifies the marker service state, not the file system\n- `onDidFilesChange` events come from the file system watcher, not the marker service\n- There's no feedback loop in the architecture\n\n**Conclusion**: No infinite loop risk detected.\n\n---\n\n### 6. RACE CONDITIONS\n\n**Severity: Low**\n\n**Potential Issue**: Files could be modified multiple times between event emissions:\n- Event 1: File deleted at T1\n- Event 2: File recreated at T2 with diagnostics from ext host\n\n**Analysis**: This is expected behavior:\n- When a file is deleted, its diagnostics are cleared\n- When the same file is recreated, extensions re-report diagnostics\n- No data loss or corruption occurs\n\nThis is the intended design pattern and matches how `mainThreadFileSystemEventService.ts` handles file changes (which simply forwards events without trying to maintain consistency).\n\n---\n\n### 7. OWNER COLLECTION PATTERN\n\n**Severity: Low**\n\nThe pattern of collecting owners in a `Set<string>` is used:\n\n```typescript\nconst owners = new Set<string>();\nfor (const marker of markers) {\n    owners.add(marker.owner);\n}\nfor (const owner of owners) {\n    this._markerService.changeOne(owner, canonicalUri, []);\n}\n```\n\n**Analysis**: \n- This pattern is NOT used elsewhere in the file (comparing line 38 which uses `forEach`)\n- However, it's the correct approach here because you need to deduplicate owners before clearing\n- Using a Set is idiomatic JavaScript and VS Code-standard\n- The pattern matches how other VS Code code deduplicates items for batch operations\n\n**Note**: The `rawAdded` field is **not** processed, which appears intentional (newly created files have no existing diagnostics to clear).\n\n---\n\n### 8. CRITICAL: Duplicate Code Pattern (Copy-Paste Issue)\n\n**Severity: High**\n\nThe `deleted` (lines 62-75) and `updated` (lines 79-92) handling blocks are **nearly identical**:\n\n```typescript\n// Deleted block (lines 62-75)\nfor (const deletedUri of event.rawDeleted) {\n    const canonicalUri = this._uriIdentService.asCanonicalUri(deletedUri);\n    const markers = this._markerService.read({ resource: canonicalUri });\n    if (markers.length > 0) {\n        const owners = new Set<string>();\n        for (const marker of markers) {\n            owners.add(marker.owner);\n        }\n        for (const owner of owners) {\n            this._markerService.changeOne(owner, canonicalUri, []);\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n// Updated block (lines 79-92) - IDENTICAL LOGIC\nfor (const changedUri of event.rawUpdated) {\n    const canonicalUri = this._uriIdentService.asCanonicalUri(changedUri);\n    const markers = this._markerService.read({ resource: canonicalUri });\n    if (markers.length > 0) {\n        const owners = new Set<string>();\n        for (const marker of markers) {\n            owners.add(marker.owner);\n        }\n        for (const owner of owners) {\n            this._markerService.changeOne(owner, canonicalUri, []);\n        }\n    }\n}\n```\n\n**Issue**: Both blocks perform the exact same operation. They should be extracted into a single helper method to avoid maintenance issues and potential divergence.\n\n**Recommendation**: \n```typescript\nprivate _clearDiagnosticsForUris(uris: URI[]): void {\n    for (const uri of uris) {\n        const canonicalUri = this._uriIdentService.asCanonicalUri(uri);\n        const markers = this._markerService.read({ resource: canonicalUri });\n        if (markers.length > 0) {\n            const owners = new Set<string>();\n            for (const marker of markers) {\n                owners.add(marker.owner);\n            }\n            for (const owner of owners) {\n                this._markerService.changeOne(owner, canonicalUri, []);\n            }\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nprivate _onDidFilesChange(event: { rawAdded: URI[]; rawUpdated: URI[]; rawDeleted: URI[] }): void {\n    this._clearDiagnosticsForUris(event.rawDeleted);\n    this._clearDiagnosticsForUris(event.rawUpdated);\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n### 2. GOOD: State Management and Disposal\n\n**Severity: None - Properly Handled**\n\nThe `_fileListener` is correctly managed:\n- Properly declared as `private readonly _fileListener: IDisposable` (line 21)\n- Subscribed in constructor with bound context (line 32)\n- Disposed in `dispose()` method before clearing active owners (line 37)\n\nThis follows VS Code conventions and prevents memory leaks. The disposal order is also correct—listeners are cleaned up before clearing the active owners set.\n\n---\n\n### 3. CONCERN: Leaky Abstraction - API Pattern\n\n**Severity: Medium**\n\nThe code reads markers and then calls `changeOne()` multiple times per URI. This pattern is correct but verbose:\n\n```typescript\nconst markers = this._markerService.read({ resource: canonicalUri });\n// ... extract owners ...\nfor (const owner of owners) {\n    this._markerService.changeOne(owner, canonicalUri, []);\n}\n```\n\n**Analysis**: Looking at the `IMarkerService` API (in `/workspace/src/vs/platform/markers/common/markers.ts`):\n- `changeOne(owner: string, resource: URI, markers: IMarkerData[]): void` - Updates one resource\n- `changeAll(owner: string, data: IResourceMarker[]): void` - Bulk operation\n\nThe current approach using `changeOne()` in a loop is inefficient. However, examining how `changeAll()` is used in the codebase (line 38, 116), it requires the full resource-marker structure, making it less suitable for this clearing operation.\n\n**Assessment**: The pattern is acceptable but could be optimized. There's no bulk \"clear for resource across all owners\" API, so the loop pattern is necessary.\n\n---\n\n### 4. ERROR HANDLING - Missing\n\n**Severity: Medium**\n\nNo error handling is present. If `_fileService.onDidFilesChange` throws or if marker operations fail, exceptions will propagate:\n\n```typescript\n// Constructor line 32 - No try-catch\nthis._fileListener = this._fileService.onDidFilesChange(this._onDidFilesChange, this);\n\n// Method lines 60-93 - No error handling\nprivate _onDidFilesChange(event: { rawAdded: URI[]; rawUpdated: URI[]; rawDeleted: URI[] }): void {\n    // ... could throw if read() or changeOne() fails\n}\n```\n\n**Issues**:\n1. If `_markerService.read()` throws, the entire batch of file changes is abandoned\n2. If `_markerService.changeOne()` throws mid-loop, some diagnostics are cleared but not others\n3. No logging of errors for debugging\n\n**Recommendation**:\n```typescript\nprivate _onDidFilesChange(event: { rawAdded: URI[]; rawUpdated: URI[]; rawDeleted: URI[] }): void {\n    try {\n        this._clearDiagnosticsForUris(event.rawDeleted);\n        this._clearDiagnosticsForUris(event.rawUpdated);\n    } catch (error) {\n        // Log error but don't crash - file changes should not break diagnostics system\n        console.error('Error clearing diagnostics for file changes:', error);\n    }\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n### 5. POTENTIAL INFINITE LOOP RISK\n\n**Severity: Low**\n\n**Analysis**: The code clears diagnostics when files change, but this does NOT trigger new file change events because:\n- Clearing diagnostics modifies the marker service state, not the file system\n- `onDidFilesChange` events come from the file system watcher, not the marker service\n- There's no feedback loop in the architecture\n\n**Conclusion**: No infinite loop risk detected.\n\n---\n\n### 6. RACE CONDITIONS\n\n**Severity: Low**\n\n**Potential Issue**: Files could be modified multiple times between event emissions:\n- Event 1: File deleted at T1\n- Event 2: File recreated at T2 with diagnostics from ext host\n\n**Analysis**: This is expected behavior:\n- When a file is deleted, its diagnostics are cleared\n- When the same file is recreated, extensions re-report diagnostics\n- No data loss or corruption occurs\n\nThis is the intended design pattern and matches how `mainThreadFileSystemEventService.ts` handles file changes (which simply forwards events without trying to maintain consistency).\n\n---\n\n### 7. OWNER COLLECTION PATTERN\n\n**Severity: Low**\n\nThe pattern of collecting owners in a `Set<string>` is used:\n\n```typescript\nconst owners = new Set<string>();\nfor (const marker of markers) {\n    owners.add(marker.owner);\n}\nfor (const owner of owners) {\n    this._markerService.changeOne(owner, canonicalUri, []);\n}\n```\n\n**Analysis**: \n- This pattern is NOT used elsewhere in the file (comparing line 38 which uses `forEach`)\n- However, it's the correct approach here because you need to deduplicate owners before clearing\n- Using a Set is idiomatic JavaScript and VS Code-standard\n- The pattern matches how other VS Code code deduplicates items for batch operations\n\n**Note**: The `rawAdded` field is **not** processed, which appears intentional (newly created files have no existing diagnostics to clear).\n\n---\n\n### 8. Now I have all the information I need. Let me create a comprehensive efficiency review:\n\n## Code Review: mainThreadDiagnostics.ts Efficiency Analysis\n\n### File Location\n`/workspace/src/vs/workbench/api/browser/mainThreadDiagnostics.ts`\n\n---\n\n## CRITICAL EFFICIENCY ISSUES FOUND\n\n### 1. **HOT PATH PERFORMANCE - UNBOUNDED WORK DURING GIT OPERATIONS**\n**Severity: HIGH**\n\nThe code subscribes to `onDidFilesChange` which fires for **every file change in the workspace** without filtering or debouncing:\n\n```typescript\nthis._fileListener = this._fileService.onDidFilesChange(this._onDidFilesChange, this);\n```\n\n**Problems:**\n- When a Git branch switch affects hundreds/thousands of files, `_onDidFilesChange` is called synchronously for each file\n- Each call reads ALL markers for the file and performs N+1 marker updates\n- No debouncing or batching - events fire immediately with `delay: 0` (in MarkerService line 153)\n- Can cause significant UI lag and freeze during large file system operations\n\n**Evidence from codebase:**\n- MarkerService uses `DebounceEmitter` with `delay: 0` (line 152-155 in markerService.ts) - this merges events but doesn't delay them\n- Other services use debouncing patterns: `ThrottledDelayer` (200ms), `Delayer` (300-1000ms delays in settingsEditor2.ts, preferences service, etc.)\n- No debouncing found for file change handlers in the codebase\n\n---\n\n### 2. **N+1 PATTERN - REDUNDANT MARKER SERVICE CALLS**\n**Severity: HIGH**\n\nFor a single file with N owners having diagnostics, the code calls `changeOne()` N times:\n\n```typescript\nfor (const changedUri of event.rawUpdated) {\n    const canonicalUri = this._uriIdentService.asCanonicalUri(changedUri);\n    const markers = this._markerService.read({ resource: canonicalUri });\n    if (markers.length > 0) {\n        const owners = new Set<string>();\n        for (const marker of markers) {\n            owners.add(marker.owner);\n        }\n        for (const owner of owners) {\n            this._markerService.changeOne(owner, canonicalUri, []);  // Called N times\n        }\n    }\n}\n```\n\n**Analysis:**\n- `changeOne()` internally fires `_onMarkerChanged.fire([resource])` for each call (markerService.ts line 183, 196)\n- With N owners, the marker changed event fires N times for the same resource\n- Each event triggers listeners to re-read markers, rebuild UI, etc.\n- Should use `changeAll()` API instead for bulk operations\n\n**Better approach exists:** The `IMarkerService.changeAll()` API (markerService.ts line 235) handles this efficiently:\n- Takes all changes at once: `changeAll(owner: string, data: IResourceMarker[])`\n- Fires a single merged event with all changed resources\n- However, clearing for all owners at once requires coordination\n\n---\n\n### 3. **MEMORY INEFFICIENCY - UNNECESSARY SET ALLOCATIONS**\n**Severity: LOW-MEDIUM**\n\nTwo identical Sets are created for deleted and updated files:\n\n```typescript\n// For deleted files\nconst owners = new Set<string>();\nfor (const marker of markers) {\n    owners.add(marker.owner);\n}\n\n// For updated files (identical code)\nconst owners = new Set<string>();\nfor (const marker of markers) {\n    owners.add(marker.owner);\n}\n```\n\n**Issues:**\n- Set allocation overhead repeated for every changed file\n- Could iterate directly: `for (const marker of markers) { changeOne(marker.owner, ...) }`\n- The Set is only needed if you want to deduplicate owners, but markers already have unique (owner, resource) tuples\n\n---\n\n### 4. **PREMATURE CLEARING WITHOUT NECESSITY CHECK**\n**Severity: MEDIUM**\n\nThe code clears ALL diagnostics for a file on update, forcing re-analysis:\n\n```typescript\n// Clear diagnostics for files that were changed to trigger re-analysis\n// This ensures that if errors were fixed on disk, stale diagnostics are removed\nfor (const changedUri of event.rawUpdated) {\n    const markers = this._markerService.read({ resource: canonicalUri });\n    if (markers.length > 0) {\n        // Remove ALL diagnostics for this file from ALL owners\n        for (const owner of owners) {\n            this._markerService.changeOne(owner, canonicalUri, []);\n        }\n    }\n}\n```\n\n**Issues:**\n- Assumes ALL diagnostics are stale because the file content changed\n- Doesn't distinguish between:\n  - Diagnostics from extensions that watch files\n  - Diagnostics from static analysis that only runs on-demand\n  - Diagnostics that are time-stamped and already stale\n- Could force re-analysis of diagnostics that are still valid\n- Better approach: Let extensions decide if they need to re-analyze based on their own heuristics\n\n---\n\n### 5. **NO FILE TYPE FILTERING**\n**Severity: MEDIUM**\n\nProcesses **all file changes** indiscriminately:\n\n```typescript\n// Processes binary files, generated files, node_modules, etc.\nfor (const changedUri of event.rawUpdated) {\n    // Always processes, no filtering\n}\n```\n\n**Issues:**\n- Changes to `.png`, `.zip`, compiled files, `node_modules/` still trigger clearing\n- Wastes CPU on files that would never have diagnostics\n- No pattern matching or excludes\n- Common pattern in codebase: Use `Event.filter()` as seen in other listeners:\n  ```typescript\n  // From userDataSync/common/snippetsSync.ts:\n  Event.filter(this.fileService.onDidFilesChange, e => e.affects(this.snippetsFolder))\n  ```\n\n---\n\n### 6. **UNNEEDED MARKER READ WHEN ALREADY KNOWN IT'S EMPTY**\n**Severity: LOW**\n\nThe check `if (markers.length > 0)` prevents work but the read is always done:\n\n```typescript\nconst markers = this._markerService.read({ resource: canonicalUri });\nif (markers.length > 0) {\n    // Only here do we need to know\n}\n```\n\n**Issue:**\n- If there are no markers, the entire loop iteration was wasted\n- Could check if resource has ANY markers before reading (though API doesn't expose this)\n- Small impact but compounds with file change volume\n\n---\n\n## SUMMARY TABLE\n\n| Issue | Severity | Impact | Existing Pattern |\n|-------|----------|--------|------------------|\n| Unbounded work on file changes (no debouncing) | HIGH | UI lag during branch switch | ThrottledDelayer used in other services |\n| N+1 marker update calls | HIGH | Multiple events fired per file | changeAll() API exists |\n| Unnecessary Set allocations | LOW | Memory churn on many files | Direct iteration pattern common |\n| Premature clearing of all diagnostics | MEDIUM | Forces unnecessary re-analysis | Extension-controlled pattern better |\n| No file type filtering | MEDIUM | Processes all files | Event.filter() pattern used elsewhere |\n| Always read even if empty | LOW | Redundant reads | Minor optimization only |\n\n---\n\n## DEBOUNCING PATTERNS IN CODEBASE\n\nFound extensive debouncing usage throughout VS Code:\n\n**High-frequency operations:**\n- `searchEditor.ts`: `new Delayer(0)` for search, `new Delayer(2000)` for history\n- `settingsEditor2.ts`: `new Delayer(300-1000ms)` for settings changes\n- `dirtydiffDecorator.ts`: `new ThrottledDelayer(200ms)` for diff updates\n- `scmViewPane.ts`: `new ThrottledDelayer(200ms)` for validation\n\n**File system specific:**\n- `extensionsWatcher.ts`: Uses correlated watchers with `this.fileService.watch()` to limit scope\n- Policy and configuration services filter events with `Event.filter()` before processing\n\n---\n\n## batch\n3. **Use Event.filter()** to exclude irrelevant files (node_modules, binaries, etc.)\n4. **Remove premature clearing** - let diagnostics naturally expire or be managed by providers\n5. '../../../platform/files/common/files.js';\n\n@extHostNamedCustomer(MainContext.MainThreadDiagnostics)\nexport class MainThreadDiagnostics implements MainThreadDiagnosticsShape {\n\n\tprivate readonly _activeOwners = new Set<string>();\n\n\tprivate readonly _proxy: ExtHostDiagnosticsShape;\n\tprivate readonly _markerListener: IDisposable;\n\tprivate readonly _fileListener: IDisposable;\n\n\tconstructor(\n\t\textHostContext: IExtHostContext,\n\t\t@IMarkerService private readonly _markerService: IMarkerService,\n\t\t@IUriIdentityService private readonly _uriIdentService: IUriIdentityService,\n\t\t@IFileService private readonly _fileService: IFileService,\n\t) {\n\t\tthis._proxy = extHostContext.getProxy(ExtHostContext.ExtHostDiagnostics);\n\n\t\tthis._markerListener = this._markerService.onMarkerChanged(this._forwardMarkers, this);\n\t\tthis._fileListener = this._fileService.onDidFilesChange(this._onDidFilesChange, this);\n\t}\n\n\tdispose(): void {\n\t\tthis._markerListener.dispose();\n\t\tthis._fileListener.dispose();\n\t\tthis._activeOwners.forEach(owner => this._markerService.changeAll(owner, []));\n\t\tthis._activeOwners.clear();\n\t}\n\n\tprivate _forwardMarkers(resources: readonly URI[]): void {\n\t\tconst data: [UriComponents, IMarkerData[]][] = [];\n\t\tfor (const resource of resources) {\n\t\t\tconst allMarkerData = this._markerService.read({ resource });\n\t\t\tif (allMarkerData.length === 0) {\n\t\t\t\tdata.push([resource, []]);\n\t\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t\tconst forgeinMarkerData = allMarkerData.filter(marker => !this._activeOwners.has(marker.owner));\n\t\t\t\tif (forgeinMarkerData.length > 0) {\n\t\t\t\t\tdata.push([resource,
